package.name: apphub
package.summary: App Hub — desktop host + App Store for apps that connect via the runtime bridge.

doc_split.publisher: Usage only — bridge API, permissions, launch. No package, production, or security architecture.
doc_split.agent_internal: When writing publisher docs, never mention production, host_dev, or package install.

end_user.summary: Uses the App Hub desktop, App Store, and installed apps.

publisher.summary: Build an app that runs in App Hub and uses the bridge to request permissions, read user info, and send desktop messages.
publisher.human_doc: App Hub Guide → App bridge tab

bridge.overview: When a user opens your app from the desktop, App Hub loads your runtime in a window and provides AppHubBridge for permissions and desktop features.
connection_flow: User opens your app (desktop icon or App Store).
App Hub resolves launch: POST /apps/{slug}/launch → entry_url + launch_token + scopes_granted (from server consent).
Your app loads in a Hub window; AppHubBridge becomes available (or postMessage channel apphub:bridge).
Use display_user from bridge:ready for UI; call publisher backend GET bridge/user for authoritative identity.

runtime_context.launch_token: Short-lived secret minted at launch. Treat like a password — never log, expose in UI, or send to third parties. Publisher backend passes it to GET bridge/user. scopes_granted on token mirror server install consent only.
runtime_context.display_user: UI-only { id, name } from Hub GET /bootstrap — included in apphub:bridge:ready. Not cryptographically verified inside the iframe; use GET bridge/user from your publisher backend for authoritative identity.

discovery.in_window: window.AppHubBridge (provided by docs/sdk-stub.js in sandboxed iframe)
discovery.sdk_stub: docs/sdk-stub.js — copy into your app bundle or load via script tag

context.scopes_granted: string[] — server minted at launch (install consent)
context.session_granted: string[] optional — Hub session desktop consents only (not authoritative)
context.permissions: string[] — manifest permissions for Hub UI
context.display_user: { id: number, name: string } optional — UI only from Hub bootstrap

permissions.description: Extra profile fields (avatar, locale, etc.) when the user agrees.
Show a notification on the desktop or taskbar.
Show a banner or message on the desktop work area.
permissions.user_prompt: Allow {app} to show messages on your desktop?

permissions.description: Show a count or dot on your app icon in the taskbar.
permissions.user_prompt: Allow {app} to update its taskbar badge?

manifest.permissions: string[] — bridge scopes (user.read, desktop.notify, …). Shown on install and DEV review; Hub UI only. Server records consent on POST /apps/{slug}/bridge-consents from manifest (never from client scope list).
manifest.api_urls: string[] optional — publisher tool backend URL(s) allowed to call bridge HTTP. TCP client IP must match DNS-pinned IPs stored at publish (production) or resolve live DNS (local dev). localhost/loopback rejected on publish in production.
manifest.api_base_url: string optional — legacy single URL; merged into api_urls.

display_only.use_for: Greetings, labels, UI copy inside the app iframe
display_only.do_not_use_for: Authorization, audit, or calling your publisher API as this user

authoritative.source: GET bridge/user (launch token + user.read) or POST verify-launch-token on publisher server
authoritative.requires: user.read or user.profile scope on launch token

getDisplayUser.description: UI-only user snapshot from bridge:ready context (Hub bootstrap). No HTTP call. Not for auth.

requestPermission.description: Hub UI consent for desktop scopes during the session (notify, message, badge). user.* scopes are granted at launch from install permissions — not via runtime bridge calls.

sendDesktopMessage.description: Show a banner or overlay on the desktop.

setTaskbarBadge.description: Set or clear taskbar badge on your app button.

bridge.example:
// After apphub:bridge:ready
const bridge = window.AppHubBridge
const display = bridge.getDisplayUser() // UI greeting — no API
console.log('Hello', display?.name)
// Authoritative user: GET bridge/user from your publisher backend (see http_companion)
await bridge.requestPermission('desktop.message')
await bridge.sendDesktopMessage({ type: 'banner', title: 'Hello', body: `Welcome ${display?.name}`, duration_ms: 5000 })

http_companion.note: Call GET bridge/user from your publisher tool backend (server-side) or via a dev proxy on api_urls. App Hub matches the caller TCP IP to manifest api_urls (DNS). Browser apps should fetch your backend, not App Hub directly. Hub postMessage is for permissions/UI only.

deploy.hosted: Build dist/ → zip with manifest.json (runtime_type: hosted) → drop on Hub desktop → POST /apps/register (draft) → DEV approves → active in store.
deploy.local: Drop a local manifest on the desktop for private install (no server listing).
deploy.iframe: Self-hosted entry_url — register via host backend when iframe listing API is available.

steps: Open App Hub Guide → App bridge, or read audiences.publisher.bridge here.
Listen for apphub:bridge:ready in your app.
Call requestPermission for desktop scopes as needed.
Use sendDesktopMessage, notify, setTaskbarBadge when granted.
Handle denied permissions gracefully in your UI.

agent.instructions: Publisher docs = usage only (bridge). Security and package details are host_dev / backend_security — never put those in publisher-facing text.

host_dev.summary: Host team embeds App Hub in the product. Owns backend endpoint security.

backend_security.principle: Publisher apps are untrusted USER-tier. Endpoint layer enforces isolation — publishers never see this section.
backend_security.publisher_isolation: Apps run in Hub windows. Only bridge routes and scoped launch_token. No host DB, env, or admin APIs.
endpoint_rules: GET integration-docs/internal requires X-AppHub-Host-Access matching APPHUB_HOST_ACCESS_SECRET (not packages-core zone/server manager).
POST /apps/{slug}/install-intent mints short-lived token when permission dialog opens.
POST /apps/{slug}/bridge-consents records manifest permissions after Accept (requires intent_token).
POST /apps/{slug}/launch mints launch_token with scopes_granted from server consent records only.
Validate launch_token per app slug and session; user_id from auth, not client headers.
Filter bridge/user fields by granted scopes and packages-core zones.
Reject bridge calls without matching scope consent record.

routes.description: Mint short-lived launch token and return entry_url. Body optional: { version? }. scopes_granted on token come from server install consent — never from client.
Tool backend verifies launch_token once. Body: { launch_token, app_slug? }. Caller IP must match manifest api_urls host. Returns user_id + scopes.
Log app usage. Body: { action: app_open|error, metadata? }.
Server-side GET to app healthcheck_url. Requires launch permission. Returns { ok, status, latency_ms, error? }.
Publisher-safe integration contract (audiences.publisher + bridge routes only).
Full contract — host integrator only (X-AppHub-Host-Access + APPHUB_HOST_ACCESS_SECRET).
Catalog. Query: mode=store|draft|publisher (default store). publisher = owner draft+active. cursor?, per_page?. meta: has_more, next_cursor.
Hub session bootstrap.
Mint short-lived install intent when permission dialog opens. Body optional: { version? }.
Record install consent after Accept. Body: { intent_token, version? }. Server writes manifest permissions — client must not send scope list.
Scoped user info. Headers: X-AppHub-Launch-Token, X-AppHub-App-Slug.
Desktop message. Requires desktop.message scope.
All apps including disabled — APPHUB_DEV_USER_IDS only.
Set app status (draft|active|disabled). Dev users only.
Emergency disable app. Dev users only.
Hosted publish: multipart bundle zip (manifest.json inside). Creates or upgrades draft app.
Version history for hosted app (semver, hash, uploaded_at).
Serve hosted bundle files. launch_token query on index; HttpOnly cookie for assets.
DEV review: file tree + bundle hash for hosted app.
DEV review: read text source file from hosted bundle. Query: path.
